Revenue saw a modest 0.8% increase to Rs 2,836 crore. The company also recommended a dividend of Rs 12.50 per share. Full-year profit surged 89% to Rs 558 crore, with a notable reduction in its debt-to-equity ratio.

articles.title: The game that makes me actually want to exercise
articles.description:
Hi, friends! Welcome to Installer No. 127, your guide to the best and Verge-iest stuff in the world. (If you're new here, welcome, my Scorpion challenges your Sub-Zero to a duel, and also you can read all the old editions at the Installer homepage.) 
This week, I've been reading about Hasan Piker and lines and David Sacks and sleep learning, catching up on Andor (for me) and Young Jedi Adventures (for my toddler), poring over thousands of new Artemis II photos, listening to many hours of possibly the longest YouTube video ever, taking more walks thanks to Pedometer++ 8.0, losing my mind during every second of every Arsenal match, and spendi …
Read the full story at The Verge.

articles.title: Quantum dot TVs beat RGB LED TVs, says the company that makes QDs for TVs
articles.description:
RGB LED technology might be everyone’s focus for TV tech in 2026, but quantum-dot TVs still might be the better choice. | Photo by John Higgins / The Verge	

At the Los Angeles Convention Center, two 85-inch TVs sat side by side inside the Nanosys meeting room at Display Week - a yearly business-to-business convention focusing on the technology that goes into displays of all types. One TV was a mini-LED panel with super quantum dots, and the other was an RGB LED - this year's hottest TV trend. Both TVs were showing the same content at the same time to highlight the differences between the two technologies - or more specifically, to show the potential failings of RGB LED backlights when compared to super quantum dot (SQD), which uses blue LEDs for the backlight.
I should probably mention that Nan …
Read the full story at The Verge.

articles.title: Sleepless planet: Why nights are warming faster than days
articles.description: Nights are warming faster than days globally, impacting crops, ecosystems, and human health. Cities trap heat due to concrete and lack of green cover, while rising greenhouse gases act like a thicker blanket, preventing heat escape. This 'asymmetric warming' alarms scientists, indicating a fundamental shift in the planet's ability to cool itself.

articles.title: Blood-brain barrier scans could personalize stroke care using existing MRI data
articles.description: When a person first exhibits signs of having a stroke, the timing of treatment is critical to restore blood flow to the brain and support recovery. In addition to studying and implementing ways to treat patients faster, investigators have been studying how to make predicting and treating stroke more precise. A new study in the journal Stroke introduces a concept of brain imaging known as blood-brain core imaging, or "leaky core," that could potentially accelerate this progress and transform the way that many strokes are treated.

articles.title: Concurrent Gainers: 14 stocks gain for 5 straight sessions, rally up to 25%
articles.description: Over the five trading sessions ending May 08, the Sensex benchmark gained 0.54%, rising 415 points to close at 77,328. The index ended higher in two of the five sessions. Despite the modest gain in the benchmark, 42 stocks from the BSE 500 index advanced in each of the five consecutive sessions between May 4 and May 08. Among these, 14 stocks posted gains in all five sessions, delivering cumulative returns of up to 25% during the period. (Data Source: ACE Equity)

articles.title: Scientists found the “holy grail” gene that could one day help humans regrow limbs
articles.description: Scientists studying axolotls, zebrafish, and mice have uncovered a shared set of genes that may one day help humans regrow lost limbs. By identifying powerful “SP genes” involved in regeneration, researchers discovered that disabling these genes stopped proper bone regrowth in salamanders and mice. They then used a gene therapy inspired by zebrafish biology to partially restore regeneration in mice, marking a major step toward future treatments that could replace damaged limbs with living tissue instead of prosthetics.

articles.title: Dhinakaran asks Vijay’s TVK to produce original letter of support claiming AMMK’s backing
articles.description: Addressing a press conference, Mr. Dhinakaran reiterated that the letter in question and a video clip, displaying Mr. Kamaraj appending his signature on the letter, were all “forged”

articles.title: Woman says ‘commuters stole’ flush tanks from newly opened Delhi-Dehradun Expressway - Video
articles.description: The woman shared a detailed video on Instagram from the expressway and claimed that it had not even been a month since the expressway was inaugurated, yet ‘the flush tank remains damaged, people took it away,’ she said in frustration.

articles.title: UK Moves Warship to Middle East for Potential Hormuz Mission
articles.description: The UK will deploy one of its warships to the Middle East as part of planning for a European-led mission to escort ships through the Strait of Hormuz once there’s a stable ceasefire.

topStories.title: The Uplift: Desmond Bryant
topStories.description: David Begnaud sits down with former NFL player Desmond Bryant, who shares how he survived a dark period in his life – and came out helping others.

topStories.title: Greg Abel knows Berkshire cold, but some miss the Buffett magic
topStories.description: Greg Abel is getting decent, if not spectacular, reviews for leading his first annual shareholders meeting as CEO.

topStories.title: Women's tennis legend speaks out on California trans athlete controversy as Newsom faces criticism
topStories.description: Tennis legend Martina Navratilova slams Gavin Newsom over trans athlete set to compete in California girls' high school track and field championships.

topStories.title: ‘It’s Not Because They Want to Be Friends’: Brilliant on What to Expect From the Trump-Xi Summit
topStories.description: The postponed Beijing summit between President Trump and China’s President Xi is back on the agenda, delayed by a war in Iran that has reshaped the dynamic between the two leaders. After a tumultuous 2025 that saw tariff escalation and revealed critical mineral choke points, both sides are looking for stability, not a breakthrough. Myron Brilliant, who recently returned from Beijing, says China thinks it's cracked the code on Trump. (Source: Bloomberg)

topStories.title: William Shatner says he's 'taking care of business' at 95: 'The least of all I want do is die'
topStories.description: William Shatner, 95, reveals his advice for aging: "befriend" the pain, never stop moving, and keep doing the things you love every single day.

topStories.title: BROADCAST BIAS: Networks furious that courts ended racial gerrymandering
topStories.description: The Supreme Court's rejection of racial gerrymandering exposed broadcast network bias in how ABC, CBS and NBC covered the redistricting, fight.

topStories.title: Connecticut pro-Second Amendment group sounds alarm on Glock-style ban, fear Democrats will go even further
topStories.description: Second Amendment advocates sound alarm on Connecticut bill they say bans most Glock-style pistols and limits self-defense options for gun owners.

topStories.title: Beet demand explodes as Americans chase health benefits in superfood boom
topStories.description: Thanks to their disease-fighting nutrients and blood pressure benefits, table beets are surging in popularity and now have a growing presence on restaurant menus.

topStories.title: A deadly diagnosis almost derailed a mom's dream. Her husband gave her hope.
topStories.description: Maralee Lellio always dreamed of having a large family. A Stage IV cancer diagnosis almost caused her to lose hope.

topStories.title: California professor accused of killing pro-Israel protester will 'likely' avoid lengthy prison sentence
topStories.description: Loay Alnaji pled guilty to manslaughter for hitting Paul Kessler with a megaphone at a 2023 California protest, and now potentially just faces probation.

topStories.title: Head impacts are associated with altered gut microbiome in football players
topStories.description: Non-concussive head impacts—hits to the head that don't cause clinically detectable symptoms—are correlated with subsequent changes to the gut microbiome in a small sample of US collegiate football players, according to a study published in PLOS One by Ahmet Ay and Kenneth Douglas Belanger of Colgate University, U.S., and colleagues.

topStories.title: Nvidia embraces role of AI investor, pushing past $40 billion in equity bets this year
topStories.description: Nvidia is pouring billions of dollars at a time into companies across the AI infrastructure stack, while also signing commercial deals with them.

dailyNews.title: Why one of the nation's largest auto lenders isn't worried about high vehicle prices or 'forever loans'
dailyNews.description: While median car payments have jumped from $390 to $525 since 2019, data provided by Capital One suggests stability in vehicle cost compared to income.

dailyNews.title: Quantum dot TVs beat RGB LED TVs, says the company that makes QDs for TVs
dailyNews.description:
RGB LED technology might be everyone’s focus for TV tech in 2026, but quantum-dot TVs still might be the better choice. | Photo by John Higgins / The Verge	

At the Los Angeles Convention Center, two 85-inch TVs sat side by side inside the Nanosys meeting room at Display Week - a yearly business-to-business convention focusing on the technology that goes into displays of all types. One TV was a mini-LED panel with super quantum dots, and the other was an RGB LED - this year's hottest TV trend. Both TVs were showing the same content at the same time to highlight the differences between the two technologies - or more specifically, to show the potential failings of RGB LED backlights when compared to super quantum dot (SQD), which uses blue LEDs for the backlight.
I should probably mention that Nan …
Read the full story at The Verge.

dailyNews.title: An expert on Iranian politics reviews the status of negotiations to end the war on Iran
dailyNews.description: NPR's Elissa Nadworny talks to Mehrzad Boroujerdi of the Missouri University of Science and Technology about the status of the Trump Administration's negotiations to end the war on Iran.

dailyNews.title: Audio captures moment Frontier plane struck pedestrian on runway
dailyNews.description: The plane was evacuated because of smoke in the aircraft after the collision, according to the flight crew.

dailyNews.title: Indonesian rescuers find 1 body after volcano eruption as search continues for 2 more
dailyNews.description: Rescuers on Saturday recovered the body of an Indonesian woman who was caught in a volcanic eruption at Mount Dukono on Indonesia’s remote island of Halmahera a day earlier, as search operations continued to find the bodies of two Singaporeans

dailyNews.title: Workers for Xbox studio Double Fine are forming a union
dailyNews.description: The studio behind Psychonauts is organizing with the Communications Workers of America.

dailyNews.title: US military kills two people in new eastern Pacific Ocean attack
dailyNews.description: The killings mark the third attack in May alone by the US military in the eastern Pacific Ocean.

dailyNews.title: Hackable Robot Lawn Mower Unlocks a New Nightmare
dailyNews.description: Plus: Meta officially kills encrypted Instagram DMs, the Trump administration targets “violent left wing extremists,” leaked documents reveal Russia's school for elite hackers, and more.

dailyNews.title: U.S. sanctions companies and individuals in the Middle East and China for helping Iran
dailyNews.description: The moves target 11 entities and three individuals based in Iran, China, Belarus and the United Arab Emirates.

dailyNews.title: DOJ sees fallout after push to prosecute former FBI director James Comey
dailyNews.description: Several prosecutors have left the Justice Department, others are considering doing so, and at least one major case has been disrupted.

dailyNews.title: Southern Republicans redistrict after Supreme Court rules, Dems lose big in Virginia
dailyNews.description: The Supreme Court weakened minority voting rights and prompted Republicans in four states to move to redistrict as part of Trump's push. A court nullified Democratic redistricting in Virginia.

dailyNews.title: How the 1994 World Cup kicked off America's love affair with soccer
dailyNews.description: Soccer — or football as it's known around the globe — was far from mainstream in the U.S. leading up to the 1994 World Cup. But in the end, the tournament was considered a resounding success. How exactly did that happen?

topStories.title: ‘It’s Not Because They Want to Be Friends’: Brilliant on What to Expect From the Trump-Xi Summit
topStories.description: The postponed Beijing summit between President Trump and China’s President Xi is back on the agenda, delayed by a war in Iran that has reshaped the dynamic between the two leaders. After a tumultuous 2025 that saw tariff escalation and revealed critical mineral choke points, both sides are looking for stability, not a breakthrough. Myron Brilliant, who recently returned from Beijing, says China thinks it's cracked the code on Trump. (Source: Bloomberg)

dailyNews.title: 6 videos that stood out in the Pentagon's new UFO drop
dailyNews.description: The Pentagon on Friday began releasing hundreds of files on UFOs after President Trump ordered the documents to be made public. CBS News Pentagon reporter Eleanor Watson reviewed the 28 videos in the first batch of files and chose six that stood out to her the most.

dailyNews.title: Trump says UFO files released to maximize transparency
dailyNews.description: Defense Secretary Pete Hegseth said it's time for the American people see it for themselves, as the Pentagon started releasing previously classified documents related to UFOs and UAPs. CBS News Pentagon reporter Eleanor Watson has more.

dailyNews.title: U.S. and Iran exchange fire as Trump says ceasefire still holds
dailyNews.description: U.S. fighter jets struck two Iranian oil tankers accused of violating a U.S. blockade in the Strait of Hormuz, as Iran launched new missile and drone attacks targeting the United Arab Emirates. NBC News’ Courtney Kube reports.

dailyNews.title: Saudis feared Trump’s Project Freedom would spur Iran to attack, officials say
dailyNews.description: Saudi officials were angered by President Donald Trump’s surprise announcement that the U.S. would start guiding ships through the Strait of Hormuz, fearing the move could spur more Iranian attacks on Gulf allies.

dailyNews.title: Trump considering firing FDA head Dr. Marty Makary, source says
dailyNews.description: Though a final decision has not been made, a source familiar with the matter says President Trump is considering firing Dr. Marty Makary after frustration with his performance as FDA commissioner. NBC News’ Anne Thompson explains.

dailyNews.description: U.S., Iran exchange strikes as Trump says ceasefire holds

dailyNews.title: Iran focus at Trump-Xi summit may delay progress on tariffs, rare earths
dailyNews.description: Thanks to the Iran war, big issues for U.S. businesses like rare earths and supply chains may take a back seat on the agenda for the Trump-Xi summit.

dailyNews.title: Trump's $1 million 'Gold Card' fails to catch on among the world's wealthy
dailyNews.description: President Donald Trump's "Gold Card," which promised U.S. residency in "record time," has been dogged by delays and legal questions.

dailyNews.title: Whisky business: Investors pin hopes on Trump’s Scotch tariff reversal after dire three years
dailyNews.description: President Trump's decision to ditch the 10% tariff on Scotch whisky could boost the market for premium cask collecting.

dailyNews.title: WATCH:  UFC champion 'very blessed' to fight at the White House
dailyNews.description: Undefeated lightweight champion Ilia Tuporia says UFC Freedom 250 will be the "biggest event in sports history."

dailyNews.title: Trump administration targets 12 foreign-born Americans for denaturalization
dailyNews.description: The Trump administration is seeking to revoke the citizenship of 12 foreign-born Americans who officials say committed crimes that qualify them for denaturalization.

dailyNews.title: Doctor explains how hantavirus, COVID-19 are different
dailyNews.description: Dr. Ashish Jha, former White House COVID-19 response coordinator, answered questions about hantavirus following a cruise ship outbreak.
